title: Choose engine by write and query shape
description: Match the TSDB (TimescaleDB vs InfluxDB vs Prometheus) to whether the workload is relational-join, high-ingest, or pull-based metrics.
body: Use when selecting a time-series store. Do not default to one engine for all cases — TimescaleDB suits SQL/joins on time data, InfluxDB suits high-ingest event streams, Prometheus suits pull-based service metrics with short retention. Actions: characterize ingest rate, query patterns (joins vs rollups), and retention need; compare against each engine's model. Validation signals include a prototype sustaining target ingest and query latency. Risk boundary: migrating engines later is costly — record the decision and its assumptions.
